rationale: The query's core transferable method is a self-consistent balance equation for the critical threshold of a binary-state model on networks with arbitrary degree distributions, governed by moments of P(k). The candidate instead uses spectral analysis of the adjacency matrix under node removal (percolation theory), a different mathematical machinery. The candidate's object—directed networks under node removal—is genuinely different from the query's Ising model on random graphs, aligning with the target field of power systems engineering.
